title: New shapes
subtitle: Letters Б Г Д З И Й Л П Ф

vocabulary.exampleMeaning: The book is on the floor.

hook.text: You've handled the familiar letters and the false friends. Now you meet nine letters with no English lookalike at all — completely new shapes. Some are logical (Б is almost like B), some are surprising (Д looks like a little house). By the end of this lesson you'll have 21 of 33 Cyrillic letters.

why.text: These nine letters complete the core consonants of Russian. Most represent sounds that exist in English — B, G, D, Z, L, P, F, and two Y-sounds. Learning their shapes now means you can read the majority of common Russian words before you even finish the alphabet.

cards.title: Letters with recognisable sounds
cards.body: These letters make sounds you know — their shapes are just new. Focus on connecting each shape to its sound, not to any English letter it might vaguely resemble.

examples.meaning: like 'b' in 'bat' — similar to B but with a tail
like 'g' in 'go' — looks like an upside-down L
like 'd' in 'dog' — looks like a little house
like 'z' in 'zoo' — looks like the number 3
like 'l' in 'let' — looks like an inverted V
like 'p' in 'pen' — looks like a goalpost or gate
like 'f' in 'fox' — looks like the Greek phi (φ)

cards.title: И and Й — the two I-sounds
cards.body: These two deserve special attention because they look almost identical but function differently. И is a vowel (a pure 'ee' sound). Й is a consonant — a very short 'y' sound, always attached to the vowel before it. Think of Й as the 'y' at the end of 'boy'.

cards.title: Reading with all 21 letters
cards.body: You now know 21 of 33 Cyrillic letters. Try reading these words — every letter should be recognisable.

questions.explanation: П = P. It looks like a goalpost and makes a P sound. Don't confuse it with Р (which makes an R sound).

questions.prompt: What is the difference between И and Й?
options: И is a vowel ('ee'), Й is a short consonant ('y')
И is a consonant, Й is a vowel
И is uppercase, Й is lowercase
questions.answer: И is a vowel ('ee'), Й is a short consonant ('y')
questions.explanation: И = 'ee' (vowel). Й = short 'y' (consonant). Й has a breve mark above it.
дом (dom) = house / home. Д=d, О=o, М=m.

cards.title: Voicing pairs
cards.body: Russian consonants often come in voiced/voiceless pairs. Б/П, Г/К, Д/Т, З/С, В/Ф are pairs where one is voiced (your vocal cords vibrate) and one is voiceless (they don't). Russian has a rule: at the end of a word, a voiced consonant becomes its voiceless pair. So год (year) is pronounced 'got' at the end.

examples.meaning: year — Д sounds like Т at word end
mushroom — Б sounds like П at word end
knife — Ж sounds like Ш at word end (preview)

cards.title: Й in common words
cards.body: Й appears most often after a vowel, turning it into a diphthong. You've already seen мой (my). It also appears at the start of some words borrowed from other languages.

questions.explanation: Б = B. It looks like the number 6 and makes a B sound.

questions.prompt: Read this word and type its meaning: лес

questions.explanation: зал (zal) = hall / room. З=z (looks like 3), А=a, Л=l.

questions.prompt: Which letter makes a Z sound?
questions.explanation: З = Z. It looks like the number 3 and makes a Z sound.

questions.prompt: What happens to voiced consonants at the end of a Russian word?
options: They become their voiceless pair (e.g. Д→Т)
questions.answer: They become their voiceless pair (e.g. Д→Т)
questions.explanation: Word-final devoicing: Б→П, В→Ф, Г→К, Д→Т, З→С. So год sounds like 'got'.
бок (bok) = side / flank. Б=b, О=o, К=k.

rules: Б=B, Г=G, Д=D, З=Z, И=EE, Й=short Y, Л=L, П=P, Ф=F
И is a vowel; Й is a short consonant — they look similar but function differently
Word-final devoicing: voiced consonants (Б Г Д З В) become voiceless at word end
You now know 21 of 33 Cyrillic letters
summary.nextLesson: Next: the hissing and hushing consonants — Ж Ц Ч Ш Щ — sounds that don't exist in English.

questions.prompt: год is pronounced 'got' at the end. Why?
options: Because Д devoices to Т at word end
questions.answer: Because Д devoices to Т at word end

options: A short consonant like the 'y' in 'boy'
questions.answer: A short consonant like the 'y' in 'boy'
